---
title: "Switchers-India"
output: 
  html_document: 
    keep_md: true
date: "2026-03-24"
---
This code is to merge polling station (PS) level election data and mergefiles from 2009 and 2014 with census indicators, SHRUG data, migration data, political party data, and survey data from 2009 and 2014. 

```{r Load libraries, message = FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(foreign)
library(xtable)
library(haven)
```

```{r Jensenius et al. Code to merge PS-level election data and mergefiles with 2009 census indicators}

# Merge PS-level data (2009)
fileshere <- list.files("data/jensenius_et_al/PSdata_2009",
                        pattern = ".csv",
                        recursive = TRUE,
                        full.names = TRUE
                        )

for (h in 1:length(fileshere)) {
  onefile <- read.csv(fileshere[h])
  
  if (h == 1) {
    PSdata_2009 <- onefile
  } else {
    PSdata_2009 <- rbind(PSdata_2009, onefile)
  }
}

# Generate summary statistics
dim(PSdata_2009)
summary(PSdata_2009)

# Collapse main and auxiliary PS booths to allow merge
sum(duplicated(paste(PSdata_2009$State_no2011, PSdata_2009$PC_no, PSdata_2009$AC_no, PSdata_2009$PS_id)))
table(PSdata_2009$PS_id)

# Strip PS-numbers of spaces and letters
PSdata_2009$PS_id<-gsub("\\s","", PSdata_2009$PS_id)
PSdata_2009$PS_id<-gsub("\u00A0","", PSdata_2009$PS_id)
PSdata_2009$PS_id<-gsub("[[:punct:]]","", PSdata_2009$PS_id)
PSdata_2009$PS_id<-gsub("[[:alpha:]]","", PSdata_2009$PS_id)
PSdata_2009$PS_id<-as.integer(as.character(PSdata_2009$PS_id))

table(PSdata_2009$PS_id)
sum(duplicated(paste(PSdata_2009$State_no2011, PSdata_2009$PC_no, PSdata_2009$AC_no, PSdata_2009$PS_id)))

sum.rm.na.logical <- function(x){
    # sums logical vector, treating NA's as 0 unless all NA the NA is returned      
    res <- if(all(is.na(x))) NA else sum(x, na.rm=T) 
    return(res)
}

### Collapse data 
dim(PSdata_2009)
PSdata_2009new <- aggregate(PSdata_2009[,c(7:51)], 
                            by = list(
                              PSdata_2009$State_no2011, PSdata_2009$PC_no, 
                              PSdata_2009$PC_name, PSdata_2009$AC_no,
                              PSdata_2009$AC_name, PSdata_2009$PS_id
                              ), 
                            sum.rm.na.logical)

dim(PSdata_2009new)
names(PSdata_2009new) <- names(PSdata_2009)
summary(PSdata_2009new)
table(PSdata_2009new$PS_id)

# Merge the 2009 mergefiles
fileshere <- list.files("data/jensenius_et_al/Mergefiles_2009", 
                        pattern = ".csv", 
                        recursive = TRUE, 
                        full.names = TRUE)

for (h in 1:length(fileshere)) {
  onefile <- read.csv(fileshere[h])
  
  if (h == 1) {
    merge_2009 <- onefile
    } else {
      merge_2009 <- rbind(merge_2009, onefile)	
    }
}

# Generate mergefile summary statistics
summary(merge_2009$PS_id)

# Create a join key
merge_2009$uniquePS <- paste(merge_2009$State_no2011, merge_2009$AC_no, merge_2009$PS_id,
                             sep = "_")
PSdata_2009$uniquePS <- paste(PSdata_2009$State_no2011, PSdata_2009$AC_no, PSdata_2009$PS_id, 
                              sep = "_")
PSdata_2009new$uniquePS <- paste(PSdata_2009new$State_no2011, PSdata_2009new$AC_no, PSdata_2009new$PS_id, 
                              sep="_")

# PS in the mergefile as a percentage of PS from the 2009 data
sum(PSdata_2009$uniquePS %in% merge_2009$uniquePS)/length(PSdata_2009$uniquePS)

# Merge election data and mergefiles
dim(PSdata_2009new)
length(unique(PSdata_2009new$uniquePS)) # There should be no duplicates, the data was already collapsed to the PS level

dim(merge_2009)
length(unique(merge_2009$uniquePS)) # There should be duplicates, as a PS can match several villages and vice versa

sum(!(PSdata_2009new$uniquePS %in% merge_2009$uniquePS)) # Are there some PSs missing from the merge file?

sum(!(merge_2009$uniquePS %in% PSdata_2009new$uniquePS)) # Are there some PSs missing from the election data? (These are missing ACs in the raw data)

elec_09 <- merge(merge_2009, PSdata_2009new[,-c(1, 3:6)], 
                 by = "uniquePS", all.x=F, all.y=F) # Keep merged rows only

# Create a variable coding the number of PSs per census unit
elec_09$PSs <- ifelse(!is.na(elec_09$Vill_no2011),
                         1, # Default: one village, one PS
                         NA)

# Identify how many villages share a PS for cases where villages share a PS
PSs <- elec_09$uniquePS[duplicated(elec_09$uniquePS)] # Identify duplicated PSs
PSs <- unique(PSs)
if (length(grep("NA", PSs)) > 0) {
  PSs <- PSs[-grep("NA", PSs)]
}

for (i in 1:length(PSs)) {
  thecases <- elec_09[elec_09$uniquePS == PSs[i],]
  thecases <- thecases[!duplicated(thecases$Vill_no2011),]
  elec_09$PSs[elec_09$uniquePS == PSs[i]] <- 1/dim(thecases)[1]
  print(i)
}

# Identify thge number of PSs in villages or towns with multiple PSs
elec_09$unique_vill <- ifelse(
  is.na(elec_09$Vill_no2011),
  NA_character_,
  paste(elec_09$State_no2011, elec_09$District_no2011, elec_09$Block_no2011, elec_09$Vill_no2011,
        sep = "_")
)

vill <- elec_09$unique_vill[duplicated(elec_09$unique_vill)] # Identify duplicated villages
vill <- unique(na.omit(vill))

for (i in 1:length(vill)) {
  thecases <- elec_09[elec_09$unique_vill == vill[i],]
  thecases <- thecases[!duplicated(thecases$uniquePS),]
  thecases <- thecases[!is.na(thecases$uniquePS),]
  elec_09$PSs[elec_09$unique_vill == vill[i]] <- dim(thecases)[1]
  print(i)
}

# Create a data set at the census unit level by dividing votes equally between villages (for multiple villages to a PS)
# vill <- unique(elec_09$unique_vill)
# vill <- vill[!is.na(vill)]
vill <- na.omit(unique(elec_09$unique_vill))

for (i in 1:length(vill)) {
  rows <- which(elec_09$unique_vill == vill[i])

  if (elec_09$PSs[rows][1] < 1) {
    elec_09[rows, 12:57] <- elec_09[rows, 12:57] * elec_09$PSs[rows]

  } else if (elec_09$PSs[rows][1] > 1) {
    thecases <- elec_09[rows, ]
    elec_09[rows[1], 12:57] <- colSums(thecases[, 12:57], na.rm = TRUE)
  }

  print(i)
}

# Remove duplicated census entries
cens_elec_09 <- elec_09[!duplicated(elec_09$unique_vill) & complete.cases(elec_09$unique_vill),]

names(cens_elec_09)
head(cens_elec_09)		
	
summary(cens_elec_09$PSs)
prop.table(table(cens_elec_09$PSs > 1))
prop.table(table(cens_elec_09$PSs < 1))
prop.table(table(cens_elec_09$PSs == 1))

```

```{r Jensenius et al. Code to merge PS-level election data and mergefiles with 2014 census indicators}

# Merge PS-level data (2014)
fileshere <- list.files("data/jensenius_et_al/PSdata_2014",
                        pattern = ".csv",
                        recursive = TRUE,
                        full.names = TRUE
                        )

for (h in 1:length(fileshere)) {
  onefile <- read.csv(fileshere[h])
  
  if (h == 1) {
    PSdata_2014 <- onefile
  } else {
    PSdata_2014 <- rbind(PSdata_2014, onefile)
  }
}

# Generate summary statistics
dim(PSdata_2014)
summary(PSdata_2014)

# Collapse main and auxiliary PS booths to allow merge
sum(duplicated(paste(PSdata_2014$State_no2011, PSdata_2014$PC_no, PSdata_2014$AC_no, PSdata_2014$PS_id)))
table(PSdata_2014$PS_id)

# Strip PS-numbers of spaces and letters
PSdata_2014$PS_id<-gsub(":00","", PSdata_2014$PS_id)
PSdata_2014$PS_id<-gsub("\\s","", PSdata_2014$PS_id)
PSdata_2014$PS_id<-gsub("\u00A0","", PSdata_2014$PS_id)
PSdata_2014$PS_id<-gsub("[[:punct:]]","", PSdata_2014$PS_id)
PSdata_2014$PS_id<-gsub("[[:alpha:]]","", PSdata_2014$PS_id)
PSdata_2014$PS_id<-as.integer(as.character(PSdata_2014$PS_id))

table(PSdata_2014$PS_id)
sum(duplicated(paste(PSdata_2014$State_no2011, PSdata_2014$PC_no, PSdata_2014$AC_no, PSdata_2014$PS_id)))

sum.rm.na.logical <- function(x){
    # sums logical vector, treating NA's as 0 unless all NA the NA is returned      
    res <- if(all(is.na(x))) NA else sum(x, na.rm=T) 
    return(res)
}

### Collapse data 
dim(PSdata_2014)
PSdata_2014new <- aggregate(PSdata_2014[,c(7:51)], 
                            by = list(
                              PSdata_2014$State_no2011, PSdata_2014$PC_no, 
                              PSdata_2014$PC_name, PSdata_2014$AC_no,
                              PSdata_2014$AC_name, PSdata_2014$PS_id
                              ), 
                            sum.rm.na.logical)

dim(PSdata_2014new)
names(PSdata_2014new) <- names(PSdata_2014)
summary(PSdata_2014new)
table(PSdata_2014new$PS_id)

# Merge the 2014 mergefiles
fileshere <- list.files("data/jensenius_et_al/Mergefiles_2014", 
                        pattern = ".csv", 
                        recursive = TRUE, 
                        full.names = TRUE)

for (h in 1:length(fileshere)) {
  onefile <- read.csv(fileshere[h])
  
  if (h == 1) {
    merge_2014 <- onefile
    } else {
      merge_2014 <- rbind(merge_2014, onefile)	
    }
}

# Generate mergefile summary statistics
summary(merge_2014$PS_id)

# Create a join key
merge_2014$uniquePS <- paste(merge_2014$State_no2011, merge_2014$AC_no, merge_2014$PS_id,
                             sep = "_")
PSdata_2014$uniquePS <- paste(PSdata_2014$State_no2011, PSdata_2014$AC_no, PSdata_2014$PS_id, 
                              sep = "_")
PSdata_2014new$uniquePS <- paste(PSdata_2014new$State_no2011, PSdata_2014new$AC_no, PSdata_2014new$PS_id, 
                              sep="_")

# PS in the mergefile as a percentage of PS from the 2009 data
sum(PSdata_2014$uniquePS %in% merge_2014$uniquePS)/length(PSdata_2014$uniquePS)

# Merge election data and mergefiles
dim(PSdata_2014new)
length(unique(PSdata_2014new$uniquePS)) # There should be no duplicates, the data was already collapsed to the PS level

dim(merge_2014)
length(unique(merge_2014$uniquePS)) # There should be duplicates, as a PS can match several villages and vice versa

sum(!(PSdata_2014new$uniquePS %in% merge_2014$uniquePS)) # Are there some PSs missing from the merge file?

sum(!(merge_2014$uniquePS %in% PSdata_2014new$uniquePS)) # Are there some PSs missing from the election data? (These are missing ACs in the raw data)

elec_14 <- merge(merge_2014, PSdata_2014new[,-c(1, 3:6)], 
                 by = "uniquePS", all.x=F, all.y=F) # Keep merged rows only

# Create a variable coding the number of PSs per census unit
elec_14$PSs <- ifelse(!is.na(elec_14$Vill_no2011),
                         1, # Default: one village, one PS
                         NA)

# Identify how many villages share a PS for cases where villages share a PS
PSs <- elec_14$uniquePS[duplicated(elec_14$uniquePS)] # Identify duplicated PSs
PSs <- unique(PSs)
if (length(grep("NA", PSs)) > 0) {
  PSs <- PSs[-grep("NA", PSs)]
}

for (i in 1:length(PSs)) {
  thecases <- elec_14[elec_14$uniquePS == PSs[i],]
  thecases <- thecases[!duplicated(thecases$Vill_no2011),]
  elec_14$PSs[elec_14$uniquePS == PSs[i]] <- 1/dim(thecases)[1]
  print(i)
}

# Identify thge number of PSs in villages or towns with multiple PSs
elec_14$unique_vill <- ifelse(
  is.na(elec_14$Vill_no2011),
  NA_character_,
  paste(elec_14$State_no2011, elec_14$District_no2011, elec_14$Block_no2011, elec_14$Vill_no2011,
        sep = "_")
)

vill <- elec_14$unique_vill[duplicated(elec_14$unique_vill)] # Identify duplicated villages
vill <- unique(na.omit(vill))

for (i in 1:length(vill)) {
  thecases <- elec_14[elec_14$unique_vill == vill[i],]
  thecases <- thecases[!duplicated(thecases$uniquePS),]
  thecases <- thecases[!is.na(thecases$uniquePS),]
  elec_14$PSs[elec_14$unique_vill == vill[i]] <- dim(thecases)[1]
  print(i)
}

# Create a data set at the census unit level by dividing votes equally between villages (for multiple villages to a PS)
# vill <- unique(elec_14$unique_vill)
# vill <- vill[!is.na(vill)]
vill <- na.omit(unique(elec_14$unique_vill))

for (i in 1:length(vill)) {
  rows <- which(elec_14$unique_vill == vill[i])

  if (elec_14$PSs[rows][1] < 1) {
    elec_14[rows, 12:57] <- elec_14[rows, 12:57] * elec_14$PSs[rows]

  } else if (elec_14$PSs[rows][1] > 1) {
    thecases <- elec_14[rows, ]
    elec_14[rows[1], 12:57] <- colSums(thecases[, 12:57], na.rm = TRUE)
  }

  print(i)
}

# Remove duplicated census entries
cens_elec_14 <- elec_14[!duplicated(elec_14$unique_vill) & complete.cases(elec_14$unique_vill),]

names(cens_elec_14)
head(cens_elec_14)		
	
summary(cens_elec_14$PSs)
prop.table(table(cens_elec_14$PSs > 1))
prop.table(table(cens_elec_14$PSs < 1))
prop.table(table(cens_elec_14$PSs == 1))

```

```{r Merge 2009 and 2014 files with candidate IDs}
cens_elec <- cens_elec_09 %>% 
  left_join(
    cens_elec_14 %>% 
      rename_with(~ paste0(.x, "_14"), -uniquePS),
    by = "uniquePS"
  )

cand_noUP_09 <- read_csv("data/jensenius_et_al/Candidates_2009/candidate_order2009_noUP.csv")
cand_UP_09   <- read_csv("data/jensenius_et_al/Candidates_2009/candidate_order2009_UP.csv")
cand_14      <- read_csv("data/jensenius_et_al/Candidates_2014/candidate_order2014.csv")

state_codes <- function(df) {
  df %>%
    mutate(
      State_no2011 = case_when(
        State_name == "Andhra Pradesh" ~ 28,
        State_name == "Bihar" ~ 10,
        State_name == "Gujarat" ~ 24,
        State_name == "Himachal Pradesh" ~ 2,
        State_name == "Jharkhand" ~ 20,
        State_name == "Madhya Pradesh" ~ 23,
        State_name == "Punjab" ~ 3,
        State_name == "Rajasthan" ~ 8,
        State_name == "Uttar Pradesh" ~ 9,
        State_name == "Uttarakhand" ~ 5,
        State_name == "West Bengal" ~ 19,
        TRUE ~ NA_integer_
      )
    )
}

cand_09 <- bind_rows(cand_noUP_09, cand_UP_09) %>%
  state_codes() %>% 
  filter(Cand_party == "BJP") %>% 
  rename_with(~ paste0(.x, "_09"), -c(State_no2011, PC_id))

cand_14 <- cand_14 %>%
  state_codes() %>% 
  filter(Cand_party == "BJP") %>% 
  rename_with(~ paste0(.x, "_14"), -c(State_no2011, PC_id))

cens_elec_cand <- cens_elec %>% 
  left_join(cand_09, by = c("State_no2011", "PC_no" = "PC_id")) %>% 
  left_join(cand_14, by = c("State_no2011", "PC_no" = "PC_id")) %>% 
  mutate(
    Cand_no_09 = as.integer(Cand_no_09),
    Cand_no_14 = as.integer(Cand_no_14),

    bjp_votes_09 = {
      votes_cand_09 <- as.matrix(select(., matches("^Votes_cand[0-9]+$")))
      ok <- !is.na(Cand_no_09) & Cand_no_09 >= 1L & Cand_no_09 <= ncol(votes_cand_09)
      out <- rep(NA_real_, n())
      out[ok] <- votes_cand_09[cbind(which(ok), Cand_no_09[ok])]
      out
    },

    bjp_vote_share_09 = if_else(
      !is.na(Votes_total) & Votes_total > 0,
      bjp_votes_09 / Votes_total,
      NA_real_
    ),

    bjp_votes_14 = {
      votes_cand_14 <- as.matrix(select(., matches("^Votes_cand[0-9]+_14$")))
      ok <- !is.na(Cand_no_14) & Cand_no_14 >= 1L & Cand_no_14 <= ncol(votes_cand_14)
      out <- rep(NA_real_, n())
      out[ok] <- votes_cand_14[cbind(which(ok), Cand_no_14[ok])]
      out
    },

    bjp_vote_share_14 = if_else(
      !is.na(Votes_total_14) & Votes_total_14 > 0,
      bjp_votes_14 / Votes_total_14,
      NA_real_
    )
  )
```

3. Merge in economic census and population (migration) data
4. Clean Lokniti data
5. Merge in Lokniti data and calculate centrism index and far-right vote share for each PS
